---
title: "HW4 walkthrough"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-05-05"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

```{r}
rm(list = ls())
```


Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

Some packages you might want
Tidyverse (for both), ggplot2, magrittr, tidyr, dplyr, gghighlight

```{r}
require(tidyverse)
require(ggplot2)
require(magrittr)
require(gghighlight)
```



```{r}
Coviddf <- read.csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/owid/covid-19-data/master/public/data/owid-covid-data.csv", header = TRUE)
```

```{r}

```



```{r}
head(Coviddf)
```

```{r}
### Filter observations to only include European nations


DFeur <- filter(Coviddf, continent == 'Europe')
### Filter observations to only include populations over 1 million
DFpop <- filter(DFeur, population >= 1000000)

```


Work with dates 
Lubridate is very good

```{r}
require(lubridate)
```


```{r}
### duplicate date

DFpop$date2 <- DFpop$date
### use date to create month year day variables

DFdate <- separate(DFpop,date, into = c('Year','Month','Day'), sep= '-')

altdatesdf <- DFdate


altdatesdf$asdate <- as_date(altdatesdf$date2)



altdatesdf$asmonth <- month(altdatesdf$asdate)

altdatesdf$asyear <- year(altdatesdf$asdate)

DFdate$location <- as.factor(DFdate$location)
### remove na values

DFcomp <- filter(DFdate, !is.na(total_cases_per_million))
DFcomp$monthlycases <- NA

#We need to sum the cases per million over the years for each month




### for April it is 15618050.024
### for October it is 8886246.167
### conditional sum to get cases per month for every year
```


```{r}
### conditional sum to get cases per month for every year

plotdata <- aggregate(DFcomp$total_cases_per_million, by=list(DFcomp$Month, DFcomp$location), FUN = sum)

colnames(plotdata) <- c('Month','Location','Cases')


### create a dummy variable if the country is in the 6 highest cases for April
plotdata$AprilHigh <- ifelse(plotdata$Month == "04" & plotdata$Cases >= 15618050.024,1,0 )

```


now that we have the right data we can create our plot

```{r}
install.packages("gghighlight")  
library(gghighlight)
```


```{r}
### generate plot
p <- ggplot(data = plotdata, mapping = aes(x = Month, y = Cases, group = Location,colour = Location)) + geom_line() + ylab("Cases per million ") + ggtitle("European COVID19 Monthly Case Rate") + gghighlight(max(AprilHigh) == 1, use_direct_label = FALSE)



p + scale_y_continuous(trans = 'log2')
```

```{r}
newplotdata <- filter(plotdata, AprilHigh == '1')


```
